requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":26,"created_at":"2024-11-16T12:17:36Z","updated_at":"2024-12-15T09:48:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56580","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`. (edit: instead of throwing an error, i.e. `first(itr, n)` and `last(itr, n)`)

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":2,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2543690602","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580#issuecomment-2543690602","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580","id":2543690602,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6XnaNq","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-15T09:48:14Z","updated_at":"2024-12-15T09:48:14Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"That could be obtained already using simpler `nth.(my_vec, 3)` no?

I was experimenting with iterators, and among other things I've stumbled into this error:
```julia
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching keys(::Base.KeySet{Int64, Dict{Int64, Int64}})
The function `keys` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.

Stacktrace:
  [1] eachindex(itrs::Base.KeySet{Int64, Dict{Int64, Int64}})
    @ Base ./abstractarray.jl:318
  [2] iterate(A::Base.Iterators.Reverse{Base.KeySet{Int64, Dict{Int64, In
t64}}})
    @ Base.Iterators ./iterators.jl:143
  [3] iterate(A::Base.Iterators.Reverse{Dict{Int64, Int64}}, state::Tuple
{Base.Iterators.Reverse{Base.KeySet{…}}})
    @ Base.Iterators ./iterators.jl:143
... more stuff omitted
```

as can be seen from the stack trace, I ended up trying to `reverse` a `Dict` (for reasons).
I know it doesn't really make sense since a `Dict` is unordered, but still that left me wondering:

Should it just be `keys(itr::KeySet) = itr`?
Even if unordered, a `Dict` is stil an iterable, to avoid having to make distinctions between "ordered" and "unordered" iterators (there isn't anything like it in the language that I know of?) it makes sense to instead make the unordered iterators the fixed point elements of the `reverse` "action"?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56548/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56548/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

see, but even if the notion of keys for a set doesn't exist, it is still implicitly used when iterating over a set by iterating over the keys of it's dict.
```julia
iterate(s::Set, i...)       = iterate(KeySet(s.dict), i...)
```

Reversing the iteration of a set doesn't make sense, I am simply advocating for covering this edge case to make the `reverse` iterator closed wrt all iterators since at the moment it breaks since there is no method matching `keys(::Base.KeySet)`.

It would make sense to have
```julia
 # following the iterate(::Set,i) idea,
keys(ks::KeySet) = keys(ks.dict)
# which would make this just
keys(ks::KeySet) = ks
```

so that the interface of `reverse` is completely general and reversing an unordered collection will just return you the same unordered collection.

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

+"""
+    nth(itr, n::Integer)
+
+Get the `n`th element of an iterable collection. Return `nothing` if not existing.
+
+See also: [`first`](@ref), [`last`](@ref)
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> nth(2:2:10, 4)
+8
+
+julia> nth(reshape(1:30, (5,6)), 6)
+6
+```
+"""
+nth(itr, n::Integer) = _nth(IteratorSize(itr), itr, n)
+nth(itr::AbstractArray, n::Integer) = n > length(itr) ? nothing : itr[n]","path":"base/iterators.jl","commit_id":"11ee27a31310d88d4606d8325eafd36fb2559712","original_commit_id":"11ee27a31310d88d4606d8325eafd36fb2559712","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"you are absolutely correct.

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-11-16T12:17:36Z","updated_at":"2024-11-16T14:13:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56580","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2480588128","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580#issuecomment-2480588128","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580","id":2480588128,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6T2sVg","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-16T14:13:05Z","updated_at":"2024-11-16T14:13:05Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> how would this compare to a more naive implementation like
> 
> ```
> nth(itr, n) = first(Iterators.Rest(itr, n))
> ```
> 
> ?

`Rest` requires knowing the state used by the iterator, which is often considered an implementation detail and hard to pick automatically (unless i am missing something!)
If the state was known `first(Rest(itr, n))` would probably be the fastest, since you alwasy do at most one iteration.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2480588128/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

 # be the same as the keys, so this is a valid optimization (see #51631)
 pairs(s::AbstractString) = IterableStatePairs(s)
 
+"""
+    nth(itr, n::Integer)
+
+Get the `n`th element of an iterable collection. Return `nothing` if not existing.
+
+See also: [`first`](@ref), [`last`](@ref)
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> nth(2:2:10, 4)
+8
+
+julia> nth(reshape(1:30, (5,6)), 6)
+6
+```
+"""
+nth(itr, n::Integer) = _nth(IteratorSize(itr), itr, n)
+nth(itr::AbstractArray, n::Integer) = n > length(itr) ? nothing : itr[n]","path":"base/iterators.jl","commit_id":"3870e3ea7aeb2f6b7c7a23608d4dcda0af057a8b","original_commit_id":"11ee27a31310d88d4606d8325eafd36fb2559712","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

+"""
+    nth(itr, n::Integer)
+
+Get the `n`th element of an iterable collection. Return `nothing` if not existing.
+
+See also: [`first`](@ref), [`last`](@ref)
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> nth(2:2:10, 4)
+8
+
+julia> nth(reshape(1:30, (5,6)), 6)
+6
+```
+"""
+nth(itr, n::Integer) = _nth(IteratorSize(itr), itr, n)
+nth(itr::AbstractArray, n::Integer) = n > length(itr) ? nothing : itr[n]","path":"base/iterators.jl","commit_id":"3870e3ea7aeb2f6b7c7a23608d4dcda0af057a8b","original_commit_id":"11ee27a31310d88d4606d8325eafd36fb2559712","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"From what I could gather that is included in the `getindex` already, since it ends up calling

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-11-16T12:17:36Z","updated_at":"2024-11-17T08:21:36Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/56580","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Hi,

I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2481024630","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56580#issuecomment-2481024630","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56580","id":2481024630,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6T4W52","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-17T08:21:34Z","updated_at":"2024-11-17T08:21:34Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"A lot of the code is for optimizing out of bound checking.  # be the same as the keys, so this is a valid optimization (see #51631)
 pairs(s::AbstractString) = IterableStatePairs(s)
 
+"""
+    nth(itr, n::Integer)
+
+Get the `n`th element of an iterable collection. Return `nothing` if not existing.
+
+See also: [`first`](@ref), [`last`](@ref)
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> nth(2:2:10, 4)
+8
+
+julia> nth(reshape(1:30, (5,6)), 6)
+6
+```
+"""
+nth(itr, n::Integer) = _nth(IteratorSize(itr), itr, n)
+nth(itr::AbstractArray, n::Integer) = n > length(itr) ? nothing : itr[n]","path":"base/iterators.jl","commit_id":"3870e3ea7aeb2f6b7c7a23608d4dcda0af057a8b","original_commit_id":"11ee27a31310d88d4606d8325eafd36fb2559712","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"I was overthinking it, I'll just stick with `[begin + n - 1]`. I've turned the open ended issue #54454 into an actual PR.
Tangentially related to #10092 ?

This PR introduces the `nth(itr, n)` function to iterators to give a `getindex` type of behaviour.
I've tried my best to optimize as much as possible by specializing on different types of iterators.
In the spirit of iterators any OOB access returns `nothing`.

here is the comparison of running the testsuite (~22 different iterators)  using generic `nth` and specialized `nth`:
```julia
@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
    for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
         _fallback_nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                           
    end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
117.750 μs (366 allocations: 17.88 KiB)

@btime begin                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
  for (itr, n, _) in $testset                                                                                                                                                                                           
    nth(itr, n)                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  end                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
end                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
24.250 μs (341 allocations: 16.70 KiB)

when test driving #510 on 'releasepreview' - 1.8.17 by installing juliuap in our cluster.

I installed juliaup and julia systemwide (read only) for users. I added the bin folder to their path and set `JULIA_DEPOT_PATH` to `:/systemwide/location`, so environments would go into their home folder, but julia installations would be found in the systemwide location.

Now users couldn't use juliaup or julia(launcher), because it would try to place a lockfile in the systemwide location, for which they don't have permission. I've temporarily allowed writing to the lockfile, but ideally we only place the lock when actually writing the config file. Running `julialauncher` or `juliaup status` should only require a read of the config file.

Related to #276","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/561/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/561/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/comments/2329431985","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/561#issuecomment-2329431985","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/561","id":2329431985,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEE6KR86K2E-x","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-04T15:51:40Z","updated_at":"2024-09-04T15:51:40Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"> Ah, I just learned something, that is clever :)
> 
> > CAS the lock file to set it to 0.
> 
> For that we would still use standard OS file locking, right? Or is that something we could implement without OS file locking?
> 
> I think we actually have two scenarios where our current file locking is a bit problematic: 1) the issue here, where someone might not have write access, and 2) file systems that just generally don't support locking (#252). If we were able to find a solution that can entirely avoid file system locking mechanisms we could solve both of these at the same time, but I don't think your suggestion would do that as is, right?

A possibility would be to hash the contents of the config file to spot any inconsistencies.
The reading could just be done without locking at all, and as you suggested before, if we read an inconsistent state we error.
the writing could be done by hashing the contents, doing work, and then before writing,
recheck the hash and see if it match, if it matches acquire the lock and write down the new version.
If it doesn't either abort with a message asking the user to repeat the operation because the config file changed midway.
In OSes that don't support locking then also the writing can be done without lock, and we go back to "inconsistent state leads to an error"

The general idea now is that `juliaupselfexecfolder` is the folder where the `juliaup` binary is located and `juliaupselfexec` is the path to the `juliaup` binary itself. And all paths should be canonical.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454","id":2293162453,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6IruHV","number":54454,"title":"Interest for an `Iterators.nth(x, n)` API?","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-05-13T15:35:45Z","updated_at":"2024-05-13T15:35:45Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello,

After searching far and wide both in issues, PR and on the discourse, I could not find any discussion about adding an `Iterators.nth(x, n)` API just for ease of use and simplicity. [This](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/package-to-read-process-lines-without-new-allocations/92448/9?u=cshen) is the only other reference about this possibility I could find.

I have played a little bit with it in the past during various projects and ended up with a slight evolution over the basic version mentioned by @stevengj in the linked post, which I am carrying around when needed:
```julia
_inbounds_nth(itr, n) = getindex(iterate(Base.Iterators.drop(itr, n-1)), 1)
_safe_nth(itr, n) = begin
	y = iterate(Base.Iterators.drop(itr, n-1))
	isnothing(y) ? nothing : getindex(y, 1)
end
nth(itr, n; skip_checks=false) = skip_checks ? _inbounds_nth(itr, n) : _safe_nth(itr, n)

simple_nth(itr, n) = first(Iterators.drop(itr, n-1))
```
which offers the ability to skip bounds checking at the expense of a crash (opposed to just returning nothing).
```julia
julia> itr = collect(1:10000)
julia> _safe_nth(itr, 10001)

julia> _inbounds_nth(itr, 10001)
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Nothing, ::Int64)
Stacktrace:
 [1] _inbounds_nth(itr::Vector{Int64}, n::Int64)
   @ Main .\REPL[198]:1
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[205]:1
```
but that offers decent performance benefits, although we can't escape the `O(n)` complexity without extra assumptions (not that I know of at least)
```julia
julia> @btime _inbounds_nth(itr, 9999) setup=(itr=collect(1:10000))
  151.222 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999

julia> @btime _safe_nth(itr, 9999) setup=(itr=collect(1:10000))
  4.400 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999

julia> @btime simple_nth(itr, 9999) setup=(itr=collect(1:10000))
  4.414 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999
```
(btw `simple_nth` also errors out when called out of bounds).

Instead of straight up opening a PR I wanted to check if there was any desire for this kind of little QOL pieces of code.
And more importantly, check with much more knowledgeable people a couple of doubts:
- is it better to just throw an error or return nothing for these kinds of APIs?
- maybe instead of a keyword argument `skip_checks` it is possible to "retrofit" the `@inbounds` macro to have something like
`@inbounds Iterators.nth(itr, n)` kind of calls, is that even a good idea?
- On the topic of the question above, is there a better way to avoid the branch in the call to begin with? is it automatically optimized away?
- Should such API just return the whole iteration tuple with the state, and let the user deal with that (doesn't feel right to me though)?
- I don't know enough about all possible edge cases of what "an iterable" is like, therefore my naive `inbounds` version only offers performance benefits in this particular case with a vector, so: is making such distinction even worth at all?
- Maybe useful for #10092?
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

  4.400 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999

julia> @btime simple_nth(itr, 9999) setup=(itr=collect(1:10000))
  4.414 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999
```
(btw `simple_nth` also errors out when called out of bounds).

Instead of straight up opening a PR I wanted to check if there was any desire for this kind of little QOL pieces of code.
And more importantly, check with much more knowledgeable people a couple of doubts:
- is it better to just throw an error or return nothing for these kinds of APIs?
- maybe instead of a keyword argument `skip_checks` it is possible to "retrofit" the `@inbounds` macro to have something like
`@inbounds Iterators.nth(itr, n)` kind of calls, is that even a good idea?
- On the topic of the question above, is there a better way to avoid the branch in the call to begin with? is it automatically optimized away?
- Should such API just return the whole iteration tuple with the state, and let the user deal with that (doesn't feel right to me though)?
- I don't know enough about all possible edge cases of what "an iterable" is like, therefore my naive `inbounds` version only offers performance benefits in this particular case with a vector, so: is making such distinction even worth at all?
- Maybe useful for #10092?

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454","id":2293162453,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6IruHV","number":54454,"title":"Interest for an `Iterators.nth(x, n)` API?","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":909268321,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw5MDkyNjgzMjE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/kind:feature","name":"kind:feature","color":"5664ff","default":false,"description":"Indicates new feature / enhancement requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-05-13T15:35:45Z","updated_at":"2024-05-15T07:59:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hello,

After searching far and wide both in issues, PR and on the discourse, I could not find any discussion about adding an `Iterators.nth(x, n)` API just for ease of use and simplicity. [This](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/package-to-read-process-lines-without-new-allocations/92448/9?u=cshen) is the only other reference about this possibility I could find.

I have played a little bit with it in the past during various projects and ended up with a slight evolution over the basic version mentioned by @stevengj in the linked post, which I am carrying around when needed:
```julia
_inbounds_nth(itr, n) = getindex(iterate(Base.Iterators.drop(itr, n-1)), 1)
_safe_nth(itr, n) = begin
	y = iterate(Base.Iterators.drop(itr, n-1))
	isnothing(y) ? nothing : getindex(y, 1)
end
nth(itr, n; skip_checks=false) = skip_checks ? _inbounds_nth(itr, n) : _safe_nth(itr, n)

simple_nth(itr, n) = first(Iterators.drop(itr, n-1))
```
which offers the ability to skip bounds checking at the expense of a crash (opposed to just returning nothing).
```julia
julia> itr = collect(1:10000)
julia> _safe_nth(itr, 10001)

julia> _inbounds_nth(itr, 10001)
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching getindex(::Nothing, ::Int64)
Stacktrace:
 [1] _inbounds_nth(itr::Vector{Int64}, n::Int64)
   @ Main .\REPL[198]:1
 [2] top-level scope
   @ REPL[205]:1
```
but that offers decent performance benefits, although we can't escape the `O(n)` complexity without extra assumptions (not that I know of at least)
```julia
julia> @btime _inbounds_nth(itr, 9999) setup=(itr=collect(1:10000))
  151.222 ns (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999

julia> @btime _safe_nth(itr, 9999) setup=(itr=collect(1:10000))
  4.400 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999

julia> @btime simple_nth(itr, 9999) setup=(itr=collect(1:10000))
  4.414 μs (0 allocations: 0 bytes)
9999
```
(btw `simple_nth` also errors out when called out of bounds).

Instead of straight up opening a PR I wanted to check if there was any desire for this kind of little QOL pieces of code.
And more importantly, check with much more knowledgeable people a couple of doubts:
- is it better to just throw an error or return nothing for these kinds of APIs?
- maybe instead of a keyword argument `skip_checks` it is possible to "retrofit" the `@inbounds` macro to have something like
`@inbounds Iterators.nth(itr, n)` kind of calls, is that even a good idea?
- On the topic of the question above, is there a better way to avoid the branch in the call to begin with? is it automatically optimized away?
- Should such API just return the whole iteration tuple with the state, and let the user deal with that (doesn't feel right to me though)?
- I don't know enough about all possible edge cases of what "an iterable" is like, therefore my naive `inbounds` version only offers performance benefits in this particular case with a vector, so: is making such distinction even worth at all?
- Maybe useful for #10092?
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2111832329","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454#issuecomment-2111832329","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/54454","id":2111832329,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM594AEJ","user":{"login":"ghyatzo","id":8601724,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjg2MDE3MjQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/8601724?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo","html_url":"https://github.com/ghyatzo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ghyatzo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-05-15T07:59:08Z","updated_at":"2024-05-15T07:59:08Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I actually think that a function such as `nth(itr,n)` is more of an endpoint in the lifetime of an iterator. 
Therefore, when you are calling `nth` you get the end result and not the continuation of the iterator. Plus it matched the intuitive action of "get me the nth element", without forcing the user to deal with the `rest` or `status` at every callsite of the `nth` function. Following a bit the principle of least surprise. 

For many intents and purposes, I see `nth(itr, n)` as a generalisation of the `first(itr)`  function in `Base`:
```julia
nth(itr, n) = begin
    y = iterate(Base.Iterators.drop(itr, n-1))
    ifelse(isnothing(y), nothing, getindex(y, 1))
end

function first(itr)
    x = iterate(itr)
    x === nothing && throw(ArgumentError("collection must be non-empty"))
    x[1]
end

# it could become just this 
# (not backward compatibile and slower, i know, it's just to showcase)
first(itr) = nth(itr, 1)
```

in my opinion the number of lines of code shouldn't matter when talking about APIs, if it's just a one-liner all the better, but it shouldn't be a justification for not putting something in, just for reference, this is the implementation of `first(itr, n)` and `last(itr, n)` in `Base`:
```julia
first(itr, n::Integer) = collect(Iterators.take(itr, n))
last(itr, n::Integer) = reverse!(collect(Iterators.take(Iterators.reverse(itr), n)))
```

installing `juliaup` through the `sh` script, and then symlinking the `juliaup` binary breaks correct autoreferentiality of the executable. `let my_own_path = std::env::current_exe()` does not follow symlinks (at least on macOS).

running `juliaup self update` incurs in
```
Error: Failed to update versions db.

Caused by:
    0: `run_command_update_version_db` command failed to load configuration db.
    1: Failed to open juliaup config file.
    2: No such file or directory (os error 2)
```
running `juliaup self uninstall` will fail to correctly remove the `paths.juliaupselfhome` folder since it will point to two parent folders with respect to the symlink location instead of the true executable","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/885/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/juliaup/issues/885/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

It centralises the resolution of the self path into a single function. Consolidated all the locations which queried the self path to call that function for better maintainability and consistency.
